Rule: Always played in double battles.
Team base stat sum cannot go over 3400.
Signature abilities and signature moves are only known by one beast.
Every beast has 60 extra points to distribute; max 30 into one stat.
A personality can shift 10 points from one stat to another.
Stage 1 = 25% | Stage 2 = 50% | Stage 3 = 75% | Stage 4 = 100%.
Health cannot be stage boosted up or down. Evasiveness cannot be stage boosted up unless done by an ability.
Blocking reduces damage instead of preventing the attack. A blocking beast will always be hit. Blocking ignores evasiveness and accuracy for whether the move connects.
Base block reduces damage by 60%.
Each consecutive block lowers the block value by 15%: 1st 60%, 2nd 45%, 3rd 30%, 4th 15%, 5th 0%.
Apply block as damage reduction after the normal damage formula and other damage modifiers.
Beasts can list multiple ability options, but only one active ability is used in battle.
Canonical override: sound-based moves use 110 AC. If a sound-based move affects opponents, it targets both opposing beasts. Supportive/self/ally sound moves keep their native non-opponent targeting.
Priority resolves in this order: +2, then +1, then normal moves, then speed order, then Chrono Shift / Shifting Reality, then Pack Tactics, then ties.
If two terrain-setting abilities activate at the same time, the slower beast's terrain is set. Setting an active terrain refreshes its duration.
Sand Burial ignores block and block-based effects, still checks accuracy, and Wind-type beasts are immune.

Monster and Frankenstein fuse only if both use Fuze on the same turn. The fused beast is Plasma/Monster, uses the best stat from either beast in each slot, and uses Frankenstein's top two moves plus Monster's bottom two moves.
Laboratory is fusion-only. While fused, it removes and disables both primary and secondary terrains, and only the fused beast's Plasma moves hit both opposing beasts.
Legendary beasts are banned in competitive play.
A beast is Legendary if it has Almighty, or if it has 700 BST and at least one M-grade stat.
If two beasts with Ancient Power are on the field at the same time, all Ancient Power effects are suppressed, even on allies.
A beast can hold only one item at a time unless a move or ability explicitly changes that.
Competitive teams cannot contain duplicate held items.
Not used in this format.
Random damage factor = 0.95 to 1.00
Moves that hit both foes or all beasts use 0.75 damage modifier
Manual block reduces damage by 60%; each consecutive block lowers that reduction by 15% (60/45/30/15/0).
Manual block lasts for that action window only.
Guard fully blocks only spread moves for that turn.
Earth Wall grants full damage immunity until struck once; cannot be used twice in a row.
Sand Shield blocks all damage for the turn and blocks status moves for 2 turns; cannot be used twice in a row.
Move-based redirection resolves before item redirection, which resolves before Heroic Cape ally-cover.
Heroic Cape has a 50% chance to intercept a single-target hit aimed at its ally.
These redirect only matching-type single-target attacks to the holder on the same side.
Confused beasts self-hit instead of acting; lasts 2 turns unless swapped out.
Stun stops the target from moving if it is hit before that target acts that turn.
Infect deals 5% max HP at end of turn and halves all healing received while active.
Blindness lowers effective move accuracy by 20% multiplicatively.
Burn and Poison each deal 5% max HP at end of turn and apply their listed offensive penalty.
Freeze lowers speed by 30% while active.
Mind Wipe and similar temporary lock/disruption effects last 2 turns unless the target swaps out.
Defense Gel and similar effects prevent selection of any non-damaging move.
